title: Pouteria luzoniensis
taxonomy: Pouteria luzoniensis is classified within the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of tropical and subtropical flowering plants. This family is part of the larger order Ebenales, and the class Magnoliopsida. The order Ebenales is further grouped into the clade asterids, which is one of the two main clades of flowering plants. Pouteria luzoniensis is native to the Philippines, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. The classification of Pouteria luzoniensis is an ongoing area of research and study in the field of botany.
uses: Pouteria luzoniensis, a species native to the Philippines, has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism.
